title: Schedule & Network Diagrams
summary: How project schedules are drawn and analysed - passes, paths, float and the diagram styles behind them. Framed for PMBOK 8: scheduling supports the Schedule performance domain (with Scope for the WBS), and the level of schedule detail is tailored to the project to keep the Focus on Value.

sections.heading: Why scheduling matters
sections.body: PMBOK 8 is principle-based and outcome-based. Scheduling is not a box to tick - it is how the Schedule performance domain (with Scope for the WBS) produces its outcomes: work that progresses in an organised, coordinated, deliberate way and deliverables that arrive when value can be realised.

blocks.title: How to read this guide
lines: Schedule domain: the schedule sequences, coordinates and times the work so benefits land when needed.
Scope domain: the WBS defines the deliverables the schedule is built from.
Tailoring (a concept, not one of the six principles): schedule detail is chosen to fit the project - light for the simple, rigorous for the complex.
The technique still matters: passes, float and the diagram styles below are all fair game on the exam.

sections.heading: Forward pass, backward pass & critical path
sections.body: A schedule network links activities by dependency. Two passes find the schedule.

figure.caption: Each box: ES|EF (top), LS|LF (bottom). The red path A->B->D has zero float.

items.title: Forward pass (left to right)
lines: Compute Early Start (ES) and Early Finish (EF).
The soonest each activity can happen.

items.title: Backward pass (right to left)
lines: Compute Late Start (LS) and Late Finish (LF).
The latest each can happen without delaying the project.
Also equals LF - EF.
How much an activity can slip without moving the end date.

blocks.title: Exam cue
lines: Critical path = longest duration path = zero float.
Any slip on the critical path slips the whole project.
Float on an activity means it can slip that much without moving the end date.

sections.heading: Critical chain & buffers
sections.body: Critical chain method (CCM) takes the critical path and adds resource constraints - it is the longest chain of dependent activities once limited people and equipment are taken into account, not just logical dependencies. Instead of padding every activity with private safety time, CCM strips that padding out and pools it into shared buffers placed where they protect the finish date.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 320 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><rect x='14' y='34' width='44' height='28' rx='3' stroke='#B5462F' stroke-width='1.6'/><text x='36' y='52' fill='currentColor' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>A</text><rect x='72' y='34' width='44' height='28' rx='3' stroke='#B5462F' stroke-width='1.6'/><text x='94' y='52' fill='currentColor' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>B</text><rect x='130' y='34' width='44' height='28' rx='3' stroke='#B5462F' stroke-width='1.6'/><text x='152' y='52' fill='currentColor' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>C</text><rect x='188' y='34' width='62' height='28' rx='3' fill='#2F6F5E' fill-opacity='0.25' stroke='#2F6F5E' stroke-width='1.4'/><text x='219' y='51' fill='#2F6F5E' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Project</text><text x='219' y='59' fill='#2F6F5E' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>buffer</text><line x1='58' y1='48' x2='72' y2='48' stroke='#B5462F' stroke-width='1.4'/><line x1='116' y1='48' x2='130' y2='48' stroke='#B5462F' stroke-width='1.4'/><line x1='174' y1='48' x2='188' y2='48' stroke='#B5462F' stroke-width='1.4'/><rect x='14' y='104' width='44' height='28' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><text x='36' y='122' fill='currentColor' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>D</text><rect x='72' y='104' width='44' height='28' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><text x='94' y='122' fill='currentColor' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>E</text><rect x='130' y='104' width='44' height='28' rx='3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='0.2' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.2'/><text x='152' y='117' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Feeding</text><text x='152' y='126' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>buffer</text><line x1='58' y1='118' x2='72' y2='118' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><line x1='116' y1='118' x2='130' y2='118' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><line x1='152' y1='104' x2='152' y2='62' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><polygon points='152,62 148,71 156,71' fill='currentColor'/></svg>
figure.caption: The critical chain (red) ends in a project buffer; a feeding path joins through a feeding buffer.

lines: Sits at the very end of the chain.
Protects the delivery date from variation along the critical chain.
Sit where a non-critical (feeding) path joins the critical chain.
Stop a late feeder from delaying the chain.

blocks.title: Managing the chain
lines: Manage by watching buffer consumption, not individual task dates.
If the project buffer is eaten faster than the work progresses, act.

blocks.title: Exam cue
lines: Critical PATH ignores resources; critical CHAIN adds them.
Buffers (project + feeding) replace per-task padding.

sections.heading: Activity-on-arrow (AOA)
sections.body: An older network style: numbered circles are events (points in time) and arrows are the activities between them. Where a dependency exists but no real work does, a dashed 'dummy' activity is used to keep the logic correct.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><line x1='52' y1='76' x2='138' y2='52' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><polygon points='138,52 128.5,58.8 126.3,51.1' fill='currentColor'/><line x1='52' y1='90' x2='138' y2='116' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><polygon points='138,116 126.3,116.6 128.6,109' fill='currentColor'/><line x1='162' y1='52' x2='250' y2='76' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><polygon points='250,76 238.3,77 240.4,69.2' fill='currentColor'/><line x1='162' y1='116' x2='250' y2='90' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><polygon points='250,90 240.6,97 238.3,89.3' fill='currentColor'/><line x1='150' y1='60' x2='150' y2='108' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/><circle cx='40' cy='83' r='14' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5' fill='none'/><circle cx='150' cy='46' r='14' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5' fill='none'/><circle cx='150' cy='122' r='14' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5' fill='none'/><circle cx='264' cy='83' r='14' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5' fill='none'/><text x='40' y='87' fill='currentColor' font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'>1</text><text x='150' y='50' fill='currentColor' font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'>2</text><text x='150' y='126' fill='currentColor' font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'>3</text><text x='264' y='87' fill='currentColor' font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'>4</text><text x='90' y='56' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='10'>A</text><text x='90' y='112' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='10'>B</text><text x='208' y='56' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='10'>C</text><text x='208' y='112' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='10'>D</text><text x='158' y='88' fill='currentColor' font-size='8'>dummy</text></svg>
figure.caption: Circles = events; arrows = activities; dashed = dummy (logic only).

items.text: A numbered circle - a point in time, not work.
An arrow between two events - where the real work sits.
A dashed, zero-duration arrow used purely to keep the logic correct.

blocks.title: Exam cue
blocks.text: AOA shows activities ON the arrows and uses dummy activities (dashed, zero duration) purely for logic.

sections.heading: Precedence diagramming (AON)
sections.body: The modern style used by most tools: boxes are activities and arrows show the dependency between them.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><rect x='16' y='58' width='56' height='40' rx='4' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><rect x='126' y='16' width='56' height='40' rx='4' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><rect x='126' y='100' width='56' height='40' rx='4' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><rect x='232' y='58' width='56' height='40' rx='4' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><text x='44' y='82' fill='currentColor' font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'>A</text><text x='154' y='40' fill='currentColor' font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'>B</text><text x='154' y='124' fill='currentColor' font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'>C</text><text x='260' y='82' fill='currentColor' font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'>D</text><line x1='72' y1='72' x2='126' y2='40' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><polygon points='126,40 118.6,49 114.5,42.2' fill='currentColor'/><line x1='72' y1='84' x2='126' y2='118' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><polygon points='126,118 114.6,115.5 118.8,108.8' fill='currentColor'/><line x1='182' y1='36' x2='232' y2='70' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><polygon points='232,70 220.7,67.1 225.2,60.5' fill='currentColor'/><line x1='182' y1='120' x2='232' y2='86' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><polygon points='232,86 225.2,95.5 220.7,88.9' fill='currentColor'/><text x='96' y='50' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='9'>FS</text></svg>
figure.caption: Boxes = activities; arrows = dependencies (FS shown).

rows.value: The default. B starts after A finishes.
Rare. B finishes after A starts.

blocks.title: Exam cue
blocks.text: AON puts activities IN the nodes. Finish-to-Start is by far the most common dependency.

sections.heading: PERT / three-point estimate
sections.body: Rather than a single guess, PERT uses three estimates and weights the most likely value heavily.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 320 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><line x1='30' y1='150' x2='300' y2='150' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><path d='M50,150 C95,150 110,46 138,46 C176,46 235,150 290,150' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='2' fill='none'/><line x1='60' y1='150' x2='60' y2='138' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.2'/><line x1='138' y1='150' x2='138' y2='48' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><line x1='180' y1='150' x2='180' y2='96' stroke='#B5462F' stroke-width='1.4' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><line x1='280' y1='150' x2='280' y2='138' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.2'/><text x='60' y='166' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>O</text><text x='138' y='166' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>M</text><text x='180' y='166' fill='#B5462F' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>E</text><text x='280' y='166' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>P</text><text x='160' y='28' fill='currentColor' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>E = (O + 4M + P) / 6</text></svg>
figure.caption: Optimistic, Most likely, Pessimistic - E is the weighted expected value.

lines: Expected duration.
The 4x weight on Most Likely makes it a weighted (beta) average.
The spread - a measure of risk in the estimate.

blocks.title: Exam cue
blocks.text: Memorise E = (O + 4M + P) / 6. The 4x weight on Most Likely is what makes it a weighted (beta) average.

sections.heading: Gantt chart (with milestone)
sections.body: A bar chart of the schedule: one row per activity, each bar spanning its start and finish across a time axis. Bars can show dependencies, and a diamond marks a milestone - a significant zero-duration point such as a go-live or approval.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 320 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><line x1='86' y1='20' x2='86' y2='160' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='160' y1='20' x2='160' y2='160' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.6' stroke-dasharray='2 4'/><line x1='234' y1='20' x2='234' y2='160' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.6' stroke-dasharray='2 4'/><text x='10' y='42' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>Design</text><text x='10' y='70' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>Build</text><text x='10' y='98' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>Test</text><text x='10' y='126' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>Deploy</text><rect x='88' y='32' width='80' height='14' rx='3' fill='#1F5FA8'/><rect x='150' y='60' width='96' height='14' rx='3' fill='#1F5FA8'/><rect x='196' y='88' width='70' height='14' rx='3' fill='#1F5FA8'/><rect x='240' y='116' width='52' height='14' rx='3' fill='#1F5FA8'/><polygon points='292,142 300,150 292,158 284,150' fill='#B5462F'/><text x='278' y='153' fill='#B5462F' font-size='9' text-anchor='end'>Go-live</text></svg>
figure.caption: Bars span each task's dates; the red diamond is a milestone.

blocks.title: Exam cue
lines: A milestone has zero duration (a diamond, not a bar).
Gantt charts show timing clearly but dependencies less so than a network diagram.

sections.heading: Work breakdown structure (WBS)
sections.body: A hierarchical decomposition of the project's total scope into smaller, manageable pieces. The lowest level is the work package - small enough to estimate and control. The WBS shows scope (the 'what'), not sequence or timing.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 340 210' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><rect x='128' y='10' width='84' height='30' rx='4' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='170' y='29' fill='currentColor' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>Project</text><line x1='170' y1='40' x2='170' y2='54' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.2'/><line x1='52' y1='54' x2='288' y2='54' stroke-width='1.2'/><line x1='288' y1='54' x2='288' y2='70' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.2'/><rect x='16' y='70' width='72' height='30' rx='4' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><rect x='134' y='70' width='72' height='30' rx='4' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><rect x='252' y='70' width='72' height='30' rx='4' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><text x='52' y='89' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>1 Design</text><text x='170' y='89' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>2 Build</text><text x='288' y='89' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>3 Test</text><line x1='170' y1='100' x2='170' y2='116' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.2'/><line x1='120' y1='116' x2='220' y2='116' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.2'/><line x1='120' y1='116' x2='120' y2='132' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.2'/><line x1='220' y1='116' x2='220' y2='132' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.2'/><rect x='86' y='132' width='68' height='28' rx='4' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><rect x='186' y='132' width='68' height='28' rx='4' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><text x='120' y='150' fill='currentColor' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>2.1 API</text><text x='220' y='150' fill='currentColor' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>2.2 UI</text><text x='170' y='184' fill='currentColor' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>lowest level = work package</text></svg>
figure.caption: Scope decomposed top-down; leaves are work packages.

blocks.title: Exam cue
lines: The WBS is decomposition of SCOPE, not a schedule.
The lowest level is the work package.
The 100% rule means children fully add up to their parent.

sections.heading: Schedule compression
sections.body: When the schedule must be shortened, two techniques act on the critical path - and only critical-path changes move the end date. Tailor the choice to what the project can afford: money or risk.

rows.value: Add resources to the critical path; costs money.
Balance resource demand; can extend the schedule.
Uses float; doesn't extend the schedule.

blocks.title: Exam cue
lines: Only critical-path changes shorten the project.
Crashing costs money; fast tracking adds risk.
Never cut planned tests to save time - protect quality and value.

source: PM Cram study notes - reworded for the current ECO-based PMP exam and aligned to PMBOK 8 principles and performance domains.
